instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `spy` that wraps an expression, prints the source form and its evaluated result to standard output, then returns the result. Useful for debugging without modifying surrounding code.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (+ 1 2)))
            (FORMAT T "~&SPY ~S => ~S~%" '(+ 1 2) #:G1)
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (+ 1 2)))
            (FORMAT T "~&SPY ~S => ~S~%" '(+ 1 2) #:G112)
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
